parsed_review.summary: CP00-240 (RP07.P02.M04.S16-RP07.P02.M04.S25; risk class A; 10 descriptor-only units in micro-phase RP07.P02.M04 "Secondary Workflow Slice") is a valid, read-only-reviewable pack. Review evidence is valid per CP178+ hardening: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit_code 0, stdout_bytes 1456, well-formed JSON result), no 0-byte stdout, no malformed/fenced JSON, no tool-call-shaped output, and no missing required fields. The pack correctly does NOT claim production readiness (manifest production_ready=false); production_ready_candidate=true is a descriptor-coverage signal only, consistent with never weakening production_ready gates. No-write/no-runtime/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via Object.freeze descriptor builders and explicit false attestation flags (performs_rollback_runtime/performs_retry_runtime/dispatches_integration_smoke_runtime/real_client_data_loaded/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt all false; customer_safe_errors_only true), verified in service.js, validators.js (CP240 coverage + service-workflow-tail descriptor checks), the search-core contract (no_write_attestation, cp240_no_write_attestation), and model.test.js. Authority boundary holds: claude_role remains read_only_independent_reviewer, claude_is_final_approver=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; human final approval remains required for the enterprise trust claim. Hermes control-plane patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime execution. Command-evidence reports 19 commands at exit_code 0 including npm test, npm run validate, build, and git diff --check. One non-blocking P3 maintainability nit identified. Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS.

findings.title: CP240 case-set builder reuses CP239 row-extras instead of a dedicated CP240 constant
findings.details: createSearchCoreCp240ServiceWorkflowTailCaseSet decorates each row with `...(SEARCH_CORE_CP239_ROW_EXTRAS[key] ?? {})` rather than a dedicated SEARCH_CORE_CP240_ROW_EXTRAS constant, whereas CP235-CP239 each define their own per-pack ROW_EXTRAS constant. No functional impact: all 10 CP240 row keys (Blocked-claim output, Rollback behavior, Retry behavior, Unit test: happy/denied/review path, Integration smoke case, Golden fixture binding, Hermes service evidence, Claude service review prompt) resolve within SEARCH_CORE_CP239_ROW_EXTRAS, none are keyed 'documentation_entry', and both validators.js (validateSearchCoreCp240ServiceWorkflowTailDescriptor, ~lines 1062-1125) and model.test.js CP240 assertions (~lines 371-418) pass against the produced descriptor.
findings.recommendation: Optionally introduce a SEARCH_CORE_CP240_ROW_EXTRAS constant for symmetry with prior packs and to prevent future drift if CP240-specific row metadata ever needs to diverge from CP239. No change required for this pack's closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Cosmetic/maintainability nit with no behavioral effect. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
